description: A designed public-awareness/anti-fraud graphic from ICE (HSI) warning about romance scams. The background is a close-up photo of a white computer keyboard. Conceptually composited: one keyboard key in the lower-right area is colored bright red and bears a white broken-heart icon. From the left, a folded U.S. dollar bill (Federal Reserve note, '$B2', 'CB 09 37250 A' serial visible) is shown morphing into / plugged in as a metallic USB flash drive connector inserted toward the keyboard — a visual metaphor for money being siphoned away digitally through online romance. Large bold black headline at top-left reads: 'Don't lose it all for love.' Lower-right white bold body text on the dark keyboard reads: 'Romance and dating scammers strategically target recently widowed or divorced seniors due to their vulnerabilities and access to cash.' The U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) seal/wordmark appears at bottom-left. Visual style: photo-composite PSA with strong red accent and bold sans-serif typography. Messaging technique: fear/cautionary appeal using a memorable visual pun (cash-as-USB, broken-heart key) for International Fraud Prevention Week. Target audience: general public, especially older/vulnerable adults and their families.
notable_text: Don't lose it all for love. | Romance and dating scammers strategically target recently widowed or divorced seniors due to their vulnerabilities and access to cash.
